skill.name: enact-hello-simple
skill.description: A minimal Enact example skill that runs a fixed greeting command.
skill.summary: Run a simple Enact hello-world greeting.
skill.author: EnactProtocol

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 4 is a false positive. The file contains a plain description and a fixed echo greeting command, with no cryptographic code, network access, file access, secret access, or prompt injection text.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Greeting Command
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 5 defines a fixed echo command. This is command execution behavior, but it does not use user input, access files, read secrets, or make network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is visible as a literal echo greeting in the only scanned file. The surrounding context confirms this is a minimal hello-world example.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 4 for weak cryptography, but that line is only the skill description. No cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic API usage is present in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 4 contains descriptive metadata, not executable cryptographic logic. The only command in the file is a fixed greeting on line 5.

content.value_statement: New Enact users need a small example that confirms a skill can run. This skill provides a fixed hello-world greeting for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Runs a fixed greeting command through the Enact skill format.
Demonstrates the minimal SKILL.md metadata needed for an Enact tool.
Provides a small hello-world example for setup checks.
Shows how tags, version, name, and description are declared.
Returns a predictable greeting with no configuration.
limitations: It only prints one fixed greeting message.
It does not accept parameters or user-provided input.
It does not perform real task automation beyond the greeting.
It does not include tests, scripts, or advanced examples.

use_cases.title: Verify a New Enact Setup
use_cases.description: Run the greeting to confirm that a local Enact skill can execute correctly.
use_cases.target_user: Developers installing Enact for the first time

use_cases.title: Teach Skill File Basics
use_cases.description: Use the small SKILL.md file to explain required metadata and a simple command field.

use_cases.title: Start a Custom Command Skill
use_cases.description: Copy the structure as a starting point before replacing the greeting with a real command.
use_cases.target_user: Tool builders creating their first Enact skill

prompt_templates.title: Run the Greeting
prompt_templates.prompt: Use enact-hello-simple to run the hello-world greeting and tell me the result.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain what enact-hello-simple does and identify the metadata fields that make it work.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt the Example
prompt_templates.prompt: Use enact-hello-simple as a model and describe how I can change the greeting command for my own workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Review for Release
prompt_templates.prompt: Review enact-hello-simple as a marketplace example and list what would need to change before making it a useful production skill.

output_examples.output: The skill returns a short greeting: Hello from Enact.
It demonstrates a minimal Enact skill with name, version, description, command, and tags.

output_examples.input: Can this skill process my project files?
output_examples.output: No. This example only prints a fixed greeting and does not read project files.

best_practices: Use this skill as a setup check before testing more complex Enact skills.
Keep the command hardcoded when using it as a safe onboarding example.
Replace the greeting with a carefully scoped command when creating a real skill.
anti_patterns: Do not treat this example as a complete production automation skill.
Do not add user input to shell commands without validation and quoting.
Do not claim the skill reads files, calls services, or performs analysis.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It prints a fixed greeting message using a simple Enact command.

faq.question: Does it need configuration?
faq.answer: No. The greeting is fixed and does not require settings or credentials.

faq.question: Does it access files or the network?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed file shows no file access, network calls, or secret access.

faq.question: Why is it useful?
faq.answer: It provides a minimal example for verifying Enact skill execution and learning the SKILL.md structure.

faq.question: Can I customize the greeting?
faq.answer: Yes. You can change the command in SKILL.md when creating your own derived skill.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only a fixed echo command and dismissed the weak cryptography warning as a false positive.
